req.title: Create observation
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can POST an Observation with a LOINC-coded 'code' element and have it persisted (positive path), while a POST without 'code' must be rejected with HTTP 422 (negative path). A boundary case targets a structurally minimal but valid LOINC code (single-coding array with exactly the required fields and no extras) to verify the server accepts the absolute minimum conformant payload, applying BVA to the code element cardinality boundary (0 vs 1 codings). Decision Table heuristic covers the combined conditions: auth role x code-present to ensure only the intersection of both satisfied conditions yields 201. A State Transition case verifies the resource lifecycle: non-existent -> created, ensuring idempotency via a unique patient reference per run.

tests.name: test_create_observation_with_loinc_code_returns_201
preconditions: A patient resource exists with id referenced in the payload
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a minimal FHIR Observation payload with resourceType, status, code containing one LOINC coding, and subject reference
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body

tests.name: test_create_observation_without_code_returns_422
preconditions: A patient resource exists with id referenced in the payload
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build an Observation payload that intentionally omits the 'code' element
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicator referencing missing 'code'
trace: R-HLT-005: error pathway - 422 when code missing

tests.name: test_create_observation_with_minimal_single_loinc_coding_boundary
preconditions: A patient resource exists with id referenced in the payload
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Payload uses exactly one coding entry (boundary: minimum cardinality of 1)
actions: Build an Observation payload where code.coding contains exactly one entry with only the mandatory 'system' and 'code' fields (no 'display')
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response code.coding has exactly one entry
Stored coding contains system 'http://loinc.org' and code '8480-6'
trace: BVA: minimum valid coding array length = 1

tests.name: test_create_observation_decision_table_no_code_and_wrong_role_returns_error
preconditions: A patient resource exists with id referenced in the payload
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build an Observation payload that omits 'code' element
POST payload to /Observation using patient-role auth header (non-clinician)
trace: R-HLT-005: error pathway - 422 when code missing
DT: condition combination role=patient AND code=absent -> denied

tests.name: test_create_observation_state_transition_nonexistent_to_created
preconditions: No Observation with the generated identifier exists (fresh UUID guarantees this)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Generate a unique identifier to ensure pre-state is non-existent
POST a valid LOINC-coded Observation to /Observation
GET the returned Location or id to confirm persisted state
expected: State transition: non-existent -> created is confirmed
trace: STC: resource lifecycle state non-existent -> created
